summary: Recent updates to the compositing and layers area of Firefox have brought several improvements aimed at enhancing performance and user experience during scrolling and interaction. One significant change is the refinement of the **Scroll Snap** feature, which allows users to achieve smoother and more precise scrolling experiences by better managing how content snaps into place. Additionally, adjustments for **Wayland**, the display server protocol, ensure that native layers are painted correctly when the position changes, further optimizing graphics rendering in this environment. These updates collectively contribute to a more responsive and visually appealing browsing experience, especially for users engaging with dynamic content.
